none: Kingston urban renewal project : environmental impact statement.

The Kingston Urban Renewal Project aims to revitalize the Borough of Kingston, Pennsylvania, after it was declared a disaster area due to flooding from Hurricane Agnes in 1972. The project focuses on removing damaged structures, restoring public facilities, and improving residential and commercial properties. It seeks to enhance the community's safety, health, and aesthetics while maintaining its residential character. The project also includes infrastructure improvements like street realignment and underground utility lines. While there will be temporary disruptions during construction, the long-term benefits are expected to outweigh these challenges.

Department of Housing and Urban Development
Redevelopment Authority of the County of Luzerne

The Kingston Urban Renewal Project was proposed in response to the severe flooding caused by Hurricane Agnes in 1972, which devastated the area and led to its designation as a disaster zone. The project aimed to not only repair the damage but also to revitalize the community by improving infrastructure and public facilities. There was some public concern regarding the disruptions caused by construction, but the overall sentiment leaned towards support for the long-term benefits of the project. Ultimately, the initiative was undertaken to enhance the safety and aesthetics of Kingston.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development
"A major problem has been the anxiety level of many people in the Borough of Kingston created by dealing with the unknown." — Michard P. Adams, Chairman of the Kingston Project Area Committee
"The concept of burning masses of lumber and building materials has to offend your ecological sense." — Michard P. Adams, Chairman of the Kingston Project Area Committee
